items.title: Biz-Tech Analytics - DevOps Engineer - India - Rs.1000-1200/hr
items.description:
We’re hiring for our client: Biz-Tech Analytics

Role: DevOps Engineer (Freelance)
Experience: 4-7+ years
Project: Terminus Project
Location: Remote
Engagement Type: Freelance | Project-based

About the Role:
Biz-Tech Analytics is looking for experienced DevOps Engineers to contribute to the Terminus Project, a hands-on initiative involving system-level problem solving, automation, and containerised environments.
This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy working close to the system layer, debugging complex issues, and building reliable automation in isolated environments.


Key Responsibilities:
 • Work on Linux-based systems, handling process management, file systems, and system utilities
 • Write clean, testable Python code for automation and verification
 • Build, configure, and manage Docker-based environments for testing and deployment
 • Troubleshoot and debug complex system and software issues
 • Collaborate using Git and GitHub workflows, including pull requests and branching
 • Execute tasks independently and iterate based on structured feedback

Required Skills & Qualifications:
 • Expert-level proficiency with Linux CLI, including Bash scripting
 • Strong Python programming skills for automation and tooling
 • Hands-on experience with Docker and containerized environments
 • Excellent problem-solving and debugging skills
 • Proficiency with Git and standard GitHub workflows

Preferred Qualifications:
 • Professional experience in DevOps or Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)
 • Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, or Azure
 • Familiarity with machine learning frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch
 • Prior experience contributing to open-source projects

Engagement Details
 • Fully remote freelance engagement
 • Flexible workload, with scope to take on additional tasks
 • Opportunity to work on real-world systems supporting advanced AI and infrastructure projects

Payment Details
 • Rs.1000-1200/hr for approved tasks (India)
 • Invoice to be raised 15-20 days after starting the initial task as long as it is approved

Apply via
 • Google form: https://forms.gle/ETNec2n1qJundZsX6

 About Biz-Tech Analytics:
Biz-Tech Analytics partners with global enterprises, AI labs, and industrial businesses to help them build and scale frontier AI systems. From data creation to deployment, the team delivers specialised services including human-in-the-loop annotation, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), and custom dataset creation.
With a network of 500+ vetted developers, STEM professionals, linguists, and domain experts, Biz-Tech Analytics supports leading global platforms by enhancing complex AI models and providing high-precision feedback at scale.
Their work sits at the intersection of advanced research, engineering rigor, and real-world AI deployment, making them a strong partner for cutting-edge AI initiatives.

items.title: Electrical Equipment Test Engineer
items.description:
APPLY on :    Email: **rahul.g@laibatechnology.com**


****Position: Heavy Equipment Test Engineer
Location: Erie, PA (Onsite role)
Full time / Permanent / Annual Salary**** 


Roles & Responsibilities:

Perform all tests with minimum supervision, involving knowledge of power and control electronics, difficult circuits, electrical measurements, electrical theory and calculations, testing operations and data acquisition. 
Develop, setup, and execute assigned tests
Attend and contribute to reviews with leads, engineering, EHS, and others when necessary to review, update, approve and perform proposed functionality tests or similar changes 
Attend necessary safety and qualification training for working on electrical and mechanical test stands.
Work on and communicate effectively with a cross functional team of technicians, engineers and Safety personnel
Provide technical expertise as needed to ensure test needs are met
Ensure all assigned tests are performed and documented properly
Attend test review/planning meetings as needed

Work Experience

Required Skills:

Associates degree in Electrical or Electronics Technology or a related technical discipline from an accredited university or college (Or a High School Diploma / GED with a minimum of 2 years of Military Technical Training or Electrical/Test experiences)
Hands on electrical troubleshooting capability and experience
Understanding and ability to work with complicated electrical schematics, verbal instructions, and / or drawings 
Test Documentation writing/development capability and experience 
Strong computer skills (Word, Excel, Office, PowerPoint)
Ability to meet physical / safety standards for heavy equipment test operations
Ability to work weekends and/or on a rotating shift


Desired Skills:

A minimum of 1 year of work experience in a heavy equipment test related field
Knowledge of AC / DC theory, Digital Electronics, 3 phase power, electric motor mechanics, electric motor theory (AC/DC), rotating machinery testing, electrical and mechanical test instrumentation, and troubleshooting electrical, mechanical, and hydraulic systems
Knowledge and/or training on Pneumatic Systems
Ability to properly use and care for assigned tools and equipment
Ability to adhere to standards and do a quality job in a safe and timely manner
Ability to promote climate of cooperation, such as; leading by example mentoring others in Quality, Safety, Environmental, and Lean philosophies
Ability to rotate through all or most of the processes, rather than being assigned to a specific task
Ability to work as part of a team and perform all related duties
 

Physical Requirements & Work Environment:

Mostly heavy industrial test environment with work on high HP (1000 KVA), medium voltage (2 KV) and high current (10 KA) systems and heavy mechanical machinery.
Frequent requirement to lift up to 50 lbs., frequently required to walk, climb, bend, reach outward, and handle materials and tools. Occasionally required to sit, squat and/or kneel.
While performing duties on this job, the employee is exposed to wet or hot, humid conditions (non-weather); work near moving mechanical parts and vibration. The noise level is usually loud. The vision requirements include: close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.
Substantial amounts of telephone and computer work.
Heavily Regulated Industries with strict adherence to procedures.
Flexibility to meet business deadlines by staying late or arriving early.
Varied Work Schedule to meet equipment delivery schedules. Weekend and rotating shift work.
Ability to use personal transportation to visit customer locations.


Email: rahul.g@laibatechnology.com

items.title: Codes Health - Founding Engineer - New York, NY (On-site) - $120,000–$150,000 USD/Year
items.description:
**About Our Client**

Codes Health is building a revolutionary gateway to collecting and understanding medical data. Their platform works directly with patients to understand their medical history, automatically requests records from past providers, and applies AI to extract relevant insights for requestors. This technology has far-reaching applications across Healthcare, Legal, and Insurance industries.

The founding team consists of three experienced entrepreneurs, each with a proven track record of building and scaling early-stage startups. The company is backed by top-tier VCs including Y Combinator, Haystack, General Catalyst, and other leading investors in the space.

---

**The Opportunity**

As one of our first engineering hires, you'll have an extremely high degree of autonomy and agency. You'll own projects from start to finish, designing the requirements and implementing solutions directly with customers. This is a ground-floor opportunity to join a well-funded, experienced team disrupting a massive market with cutting-edge AI technology.

**What makes this role special:**

- Work directly with serial entrepreneurs who have a proven track record of success  
- High autonomy and direct impact on product direction  
- Substantial growth and earning potential  
- Meaningful equity in a rapidly growing company  
- Opportunity to build something transformative from the ground up  

---

**What We're Looking For**

**Essential Requirements:**

- Full-stack generalist who thrives across the entire technology stack  
- Strong experience with React (frontend) and Python/FastAPI (backend)  
- Proven ability with prompt engineering and creating AI workflows  
- Fast learner who makes new technology useful, quickly  
- "Run through walls" mentality - no task is beneath you, and challenges fuel your drive  
- Hustler mindset with willingness to work in a high-intensity environment (60+ hours/week)  
- Based in NYC and excited about on-site collaboration  

**Ideal Background:**

- Prior experience at an early-stage startup or fast-growing company  
- Experience working with LLMs beyond basic prompt engineering  
- Background from top engineering schools (MIT, Stanford, CMU, Berkeley, Cornell preferred)  
- Track record of executing with speed while thinking strategically (80% execution, 20% strategy)  

---

**What You'll Be Doing**

- Building core features on the health data platform used by customers across Healthcare, Legal, and Insurance industries  
- Developing internal tools that scale their operations and improve efficiency  
- Supporting customer relationships and rapidly iterating based on feedback  
- Owning projects end-to-end from conception to deployment and maintenance  
- Collaborating directly with founders on product strategy and technical decisions  

---

**Start-up Stack**

- **Frontend:** TypeScript/React  
- **Backend:** Python/FastAPI  
- **AI/ML:** Advanced prompt engineering, LLM integration, custom AI workflows  
- **Infrastructure:** Modern cloud-native architecture  

---

**Offer**

**Compensation:**

- Competitive salary: $120K - $150K  
- Meaningful equity: 0.75% - 1.50%  
- Opportunity for rapid salary and equity growth as the company scales  

**Growth & Learning:**

- Direct mentorship from successful serial entrepreneurs  
- Extremely high learning curve in a fast-paced environment  
- Opportunity to wear multiple hats and expand your skill set rapidly  
- Front-row seat to building a category-defining company  

**Culture:**

- Small, tight-knit team where every voice matters  
- High-trust environment with significant autonomy  
- Fast decision-making and execution  
- Commitment to building something meaningful that impacts healthcare  

---

**Ready to Join?**

We're looking for someone who is excited about the opportunity to grind alongside the founding team and build something extraordinary. This isn't a typical 9-5 role - we're seeking someone who thrives in high-intensity environments and wants to be part of creating the next big thing in healthcare technology.

If you're a strong developer with a hustler mentality, ready to take ownership of meaningful projects and grow alongside a well-funded startup with experienced founders, we'd love to hear from you.

**To Apply:** Please send your resume to **anna.s.bielikova@gmail.com** along with a brief note about why you're excited about this opportunity and your experience with the tech stack.

---

items.title: Pitchpoint Solutions - Angular Software Development Engineer - Toronto/Remote - 50,000 to 125,000 CAD/Year
items.description:
**Time Commitment:** `Full-Time`
**Remote Status:** `Remote Worldwide`
**Compensation:** `$50,000 - $125,000 CAD/Year`

### Think Big. Be Bold. Define Tomorrow.

At Pitchpoint, we empower the U.S. mortgage industry with a mission-critical SaaS platform that has been trusted for over 15 years. We solve deep and satisfying technical challenges, from architecting systems that process millions of real-time decisions to building the intuitive user interfaces that make complex data understandable and actionable.

We are seeking an Intermediate Angular Software Development Engineer who combines a passion for clean, elegant code with a deep empathy for the user, driven to translate complex requirements into intuitive and reliable experiences.

This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career on a high-growth, remote-first team. Here, you will have the stability to tackle significant challenges, the autonomy to own key features, and the chance to see the real-world impact of your code.

### Your Role & Impact

As an Intermediate Angular Software Development Engineer, you’ll join the team building our next-generation platform. This isn't a maintenance role; you'll be building the future of our product and directly helping our users stop financial fraud. You’ll have ownership over key features from concept to deployment and work in a tight-knit team alongside product, data analytics, and other engineers to ship code that matters.

### The Core Challenges You'll Tackle

* **Engineer for Flexibility and Scale:** Architect and build generalized, platform-level solutions that support deep, self-serve customization. Think less about building a single feature and more about creating the building blocks our customers can use themselves.
* **Innovate Within a Regulated Industry:** Take on the unique challenge of building high-performance, cutting-edge software within the real-world constraints of financial compliance and enterprise-grade security.
* **Find the Best Solution, Together:** Be a key voice in a cross-functional team. We expect you to contribute ideas, challenge assumptions, and work closely with product and design experts to not just build the feature right, but to build the right feature.
* **Bridge the Gap Between AI and the User:** Build the critical infrastructure, from APIs to UI components, that allow our users to interact with, manage, and trust complex AI-enhanced process flows and agentic decisioning systems.

### Signs You'll Thrive on Our Team

* **You're driven by curiosity:** You have a genuine passion for the craft of software development and are always looking for better ways to build things.
* **You're comfortable with the unknown:** When faced with an ambiguous problem, your first instinct is to start breaking it down and prototyping solutions.
* **You build with empathy:** You write code with your future self, your teammates, and the end user in mind, caring about creating solutions that are maintainable and intuitive.
* **You balance speed and quality:** You understand the trade-offs between a quick fix and a scalable architecture and can help the team make smart, strategic decisions.
* **You're eager to accelerate your career** by tackling complex problems with a high-growth team.

### Required Skills & Experience

* **Deep Experience with Angular:** 3+ years of professional experience building complex, scalable applications with Angular, TypeScript, and Storybook. You understand architecture and best practices.
* **Full-Stack Awareness:** A strong understanding of backend development using Node.js, Express.js, and Docker to collaborate effectively on API design.
* **Mastery of API Integration:** Proficient in consuming both RESTful and GraphQL APIs to manage complex data flows.
* **A Commitment to Quality Code:** Experience writing meaningful unit tests (e.g., Jest, Jasmine, Karma) and E2E tests (e.g., Cypress, Playwright).
* **A Passion for Great UX:** A good eye for design and proven experience turning mockups from tools like Figma into responsive, intuitive, and pixel-perfect interfaces.
* **Complex State Management:** Experience with state management in large-scale applications, preferably using libraries like NgRx.
* **SDLC Proficiency:** Proficient in version control with Git/GitHub (branching, PRs, Actions), package management with npm, and JavaScript bundling with Webpack.

### Education & Fundamentals

While we value hands-on experience above all, a strong foundation in computer science principles is typical for success on our team. You can apply core concepts like data structures, algorithms, and time complexity to solve real-world front-end challenges. This often comes from a Computer Science degree or equivalent practical experience.

### Bonus Points: Experience That Will Help You Excel

* **Deep Expertise in FinTech:** A background in mortgage lending, fraud detection, or risk mitigation. Hands-on experience with a Loan Origination System (LOS) like Encompass, Calyx, etc.
* **Advanced Cloud & Data Skills:** Practical experience building cloud-native applications (especially on GCP) and dealing with data processing for financial applications.
* **Practical AI/ML Application:** Exposure to applying generative AI, predictive modeling, or anomaly detection in a financial context, including prompt engineering for LLMs.

### Why You'll Love Working at Pitchpoint

* **Real-World Impact:** Your code helps protect the North American financial ecosystem from fraud.
* **A Truly Remote Team:** We focus on outcomes, not micromanagement, with the flexibility to work from where you want.
* **Accelerate Your Career Growth:** Learn by solving complex problems alongside a talented team of senior engineers.
* **An Empowered, No-Ego Engineering Culture:** We value open feedback, spirited debate, and blameless retrospectives.

### How to Apply

Ready to build the future of FinTech? Apply directly through the link below.

[**Apply Now**](https://pitchpointsolutions.bamboohr.com/hiring/jobs/30)

We are focused on simplifying and accelerating the work flows for creatives. Utilizing cutting edge technology to give SMBs super powers.

## About the Role

We are looking for an entrepreneurial, mission-driven self starter to join our engineering team. This is a hands-on coding role with high autonomy and ownership on how to design the system, structure the codebase, and drive quality for the product. You will be one of the first 10 hires in engineering. As an early employee, you have the opportunity to wear many hats. Your ideas may shape key product features. You may need to conduct a technical walkthroughs on client calls to close a deal. Your voice holds weight as an early employee. Working alongside the founders and like-minded senior entrepreneurs, you have a chance to grow rapidly and build meaningful technology to better creator lives. 

### Responsibilities

- Take part in planning, working with the whole team to define and develop features from 0 to 1
- Work closely with Product Design to find creative solutions to ensure the technical feasibility of UI/UX designs
- Design and implement scalable backend services and integrate third-party APIs

- Understand how to balance speed of delivery with maintainable and reliable software

- Proficient in TypeScript and ReactJS

- Experience with GraphQL

- Experience writing tests using frameworks such as  Jest and Cypress
- Strong understanding of containerization (Docker / Kubernetes)
- Experience with serverless architecture

### Nice to Haves

- Python experience

- Experience with ETL and data pipelines

- You are subscribed to technology trends and always excited to bring in or adopt best practice
- You are curious to learn and never backs down from a challenge
- You get things done by managing your capacity, stakeholder expectations, and staying aligned on priorities
- You can articulate growth, product,  engineering tradeoffs to make decisions or achieve buy-in
- We believe in the use of tools, services, and vendors to augment our team and allow us to focus on our core technology
- You can communicate clearly with internal team members, non-technical stakeholders, and external customers / prospects

### Perks and Benefits

- Flexible working hours and remote work options

Our interview process includes an initial phone screen, a technical interview, and a final interview with the leadership team. We strive to make the process as smooth and efficient as possible.

- Get to work with @ahmadnassri - founder of Refactor community!

- Great comp and great benefits because we need the _best_ IT manager.

### Salary Expectation
Negotiable, but somewhere around $130,000 CAD/year + 15% bonus.

### Benefits

- Competitive Medical Benefits
- 27" 4k Monitor, M3 Macbook Pro, Keyboard and Mouse for your home setup
- Health Spending Account
- Remote-first work model
- Flexible Paid Time-Off
- Employee Assistance Program
- Paid Parental Leave
- Group RRSP and 401(k)
- VN Gives Back: Paid Volunteer Days
- Awards Program
- VN Virtual Communities

### Location
- We have an office in Mississauga, Ontario but we're remote-first.
- Our team attends the office when we need to, since we do have to take care of remote worker logistics like shipping laptops around.

## What You'll Do _(Job Description)_

We need a passionate, proactive, details oriented, veteran IT professional with management experience.

Key Responsibilities:

- Report to the Senior Director of IT and Security
- Manage and optimize the implementation and maintenance of IT systems including MDM, Google Workspace, Jira, and Apple Business Manager.
- Develop and enforce IT policies and systems to support fully remote operations.
- Oversee the security of IT systems to prevent unauthorized access and data breaches in collaboration with the IT Security Team.
- Provide leadership and direction to the IT department to ensure operational excellence.
- Coordinate with the Senior Director of IT and Security, department heads, and executive management to align IT infrastructure with organizational goals.
- Manage IT projects from conception to execution, ensuring efficient and on-budget delivery.
- Analyze business requirements by partnering with key stakeholders across the organization to develop solutions for IT needs.
- Delegate troubleshooting and resolve issues with hardware, software, and network systems to ensure continuous business operations. Go hands-on when necessary.
- Prepare financial budgets for IT expenses and investments.
- Conduct regular system audits and provide comprehensive management reports.

- A customer service attitude 100%.
- Proven experience as an IT Manager or similar role with at least 5 years in a fully remote work environment.
- Strong expertise in managing and troubleshooting Jamf, Google Workspace, Jira, and Apple Business Manager.
- Exceptional attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
- Excellent leadership and communication skills, capable of conveying complex IT concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
- Experience in designing and implementing IT strategies that align with business goals.
- Strong organizational and project management skills.


### Nice to Have
- Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field. Advanced degrees or certifications in management or IT are preferred.

### Relevant Experiences
- Terraform experience
- Experience passing a SOC2 Type II audit.
- Experience making Jira not suck.
- Experience with agile ritual type stuff without all the BS. Retrospectives, sprints, commitment based management.
- Google Workspace
- Jamf / SimpleMDM / Apple Business Manager

items.description:

# Issue title format: [Company Name] - [Job Title] - [Location] - [Salary] [Currency]/[Frequency]

-->
Our official career page: [click here to apply](https://www.think-cell.com/career/jobs/development?ec=rC0IZep7h7pmLcx8ndavN6CYqAqwS1w6rWJ8C30Q0MQcBTL97qhrOxn34Sklr6jQNODy_edu-fd-bsHwp3MXAOL7NPAAMb1hHS7_WMeGegscny4d4DTH5RQfQqnnuy5IN6MX6_I) 

## What You'll get

### Salary Expectation

<!--
  - Be sure to specify an actual salary figure, or at least a range
  - Specify a current locale, such as GBP, USD, or CAD

  IMPORTANT: Posts that do not include a salary or range
  will receive a 'needs info' label
-->

- A competitive salary from the start and a raise to EUR 130,000 annually after only one year
- Full Time

### Benefits

- An international team of brilliant minds
- A working environment that makes this team stay and grow
- Enough time to make sure that every detail of your solution is perfect
- A flat organization and plenty of room for your ideas
- No scheduled meetings
- Family-friendly working hours, no deadlines, no overtime
- Support for relocation

### Location

<!--
  Please specify your job's policy on remote work (see README for details)
-->

- Berlin, Germany
- Remote

## What You'll do _(Job Description)_
### About this job

We are look­ing for smart, cre­at­ive developers with a solid the­or­et­ical background. Our team of developers con­sists of those with 15+ years of com­mer­cial experience in vari­ous fields, to fresh gradu­ates in Com­puter Sci­ence, Phys­ics, and Math­em­at­ics. A uni­versity degree or work experience with C++, together with a fresh and cre­at­ive prob­lem-solv­ing approach would be an excellent com­bin­a­tion for this role. As all of our recruit­ment steps are task-based, the selection is based on the skills demonstrated during our tests.

You will work largely inde­pend­ently and will be responsible for the whole range of activ­it­ies when implementing a new fea­ture. You should be able to look at a prob­lem from the user's perspective and dis­cuss abstract concepts with fel­low developers. We expect each of our developers to do archi­tec­ture, design, imple­ment­a­tion, cus­tomer feed­back, and bug fix­ing, rather than split­ting these activ­it­ies between sev­eral people. We thus put everyone in control of their own work.

At think-cell, there are no dead­lines and no sched­uled meet­ings. A fea­ture is ready to be released when you are con­vinced that you have imple­men­ted the best pos­sible solu­tion. Meet­ings take place as needed, with only the people who are actu­ally involved being required to be present. At any time in the pro­cess, ideas, sug­ges­tions, and cri­ti­cism from any­body in the hier­archy is wel­come and is ser­i­ously con­sidered. Your ideas are wel­come, even if they mean that we have to change a lot of code to make things better.

We have published sev­eral sci­entific art­icles in the areas of Arti­fi­cial Intel­li­gence and Com­puter Graph­ics and we will encourage you to do the same. We spon­sor vis­its to con­fer­ences and have close rela­tion­ships with uni­versit­ies and research insti­tutes in the U.S. and Ger­many.

think-cell encour­ages a healthy work-life bal­ance. We do not work at night or on week­ends.

We pay very com­pet­it­ive salar­ies and offer our developers EUR 130,000 annu­ally fol­low­ing one year of employ­ment. If necessary, we will go out of our way to help you relo­cate to Ber­lin and will do what we can to help you acquire a work per­mit. While you should be able to com­mu­nic­ate effectively in English, know­ing Ger­man is not required. Ger­man lan­guage courses can be arranged if desired.

## What You Need to Be Successful _(Skills)_

### Must Have
- C++ 
- Algorithms


### Relevant Experiences
In fact, we have several engineers who joined right after graduating from university. 

## About Our Company
think-cell is a fast-paced software company in Berlin, Germany, with a focus on developing graphics products that stand out from the crowd. More than 1,000,000 users worldwide rely on [our software](https://www.think-cell.com/en/product) for their daily business as it makes creating graphical presentations so much easier, faster, and more enjoyable. Among our customers are many renowned consulting companies and large international corporations.

We do not have to make compromises with regard to code quality and beauty, because think-cell is highly profitable. We are willing to go the extra mile to develop sophisticated algorithms and refine our user interface, and we are proud of our many happy customers. The fact that the company is owned and managed by seasoned computer scientists certainly contributes to a working environment that makes exceptional developers come and stay.

### About our soft­ware

Our focus is on busi­ness slides (as opposed to more art­ful applic­a­tions) because they offer great potential for auto­ma­tion of lay­out tasks that are tra­di­tion­ally per­formed by Power­Point users themselves. Chal­lenges are plenty: from a solid under­stand­ing of what makes a good lay­out and which guidelines are fol­lowed by humans who do manual lay­out, to algorithms that pro­duce an accept­able out­put fast enough for inter­act­ive slide design, to a graph­ical user inter­face that sup­ports our new, ori­ginal approach to slide lay­out in a way that is easy to under­stand yet unob­trus­ive, to solid tech­nical solu­tions for auto­matic bug report­ing and auto­matic updates, to com­pat­ib­il­ity with third-party soft­ware on the com­puters of half a mil­lion users.

Here are some highlights of what we have done.

**Lan­guage**

- Everything we do is C++. Even our customer portal is written in C++. There is some Assem­bler glue code where it is necessary, and our build scripts are written in Python, but other than that think-cell is all about C++.
- We closely track the latest ver­sions of our com­pilers, Visual C++ and Xcode, so we can always use the latest C++ stand­ard fea­tures as soon as they become avail­able.
- We fund the work­ing group for pro­gram­ming lan­guages of the Ger­man Insti­tute for Stand­ard­iz­a­tion (DIN). Some of our employ­ees are mem­bers of this com­mit­tee and vote in the inter­na­tional stand­ard­iz­a­tion process of ISO/IEC C++.
- We spon­sor the Stand­ard C++ Found­a­tion help­ing them to pro­mote the under­stand­ing and use of mod­ern Stand­ard C++ on all com­pilers and plat­forms.

**Lib­rary**

- We use Boost throughout our code, e.g., Boost—Spirit for pars­ing.
- We have our own range lib­rary, in the same spirit as Boost. Range or Eric Niebler’s range-v3, but going fur­ther, for example, by uni­fy­ing internal and external iter­a­tion. We gave a talk about it, and most of the code is pub­lic.
- We develop our own cross-plat­form lib­rary to sup­port Mac and Win­dows with a single code base.
- We have our own ref­er­ence-count­ing and per­sist­ence lib­rar­ies to save and restore whole object trees.
- We have an extens­ive bug report­ing infra­struc­ture. Asser­tions and error checks stay in the release code, and our soft­ware auto­mat­ic­ally reports bugs to our server. The server ana­lyzes the bug, cat­egor­izes it, and files it in a data­base that all developers can access. If an update fixes the bug, the user can down­load the update directly from a bug response web page.

**Algorithms**

- think-cell was foun­ded on the idea for an algorithm for auto­matic slide lay­out, and we are still on an excit­ing jour­ney towards that ambi­tious vis­ion. You can see our most recent release in action!
- We developed a new algorithm for auto­matic point cloud labeling that allows labels to be posi­tioned away from the actual points.
- We developed a new algorithm for auto­matic column chart labeling.
- We are work­ing with John For­rest – author of the lin­ear solver CLP – to make his sim­plex code faster on our kind of prob­lems.
- We developed many gen­eric data struc­tures that are not in C++ or Boost, for example par­ti­tions.
- Our software not only produces charts, it is also able to read them back from paper. For our chart recog­ni­tion tool, we rely on OpenCV and the Lepton­ica Image Pro­cess­ing Lib­rary.

**Reverse Engin­eer­ing**

- We do lots of reverse engin­eer­ing with the dis­as­sem­bler IDA from Hex-Rays, in order to achieve things that are not pos­sible via the doc­u­men­ted Microsoft Office API.
- We wrote prob­ably the best func­tion hook­ing engine out there. At each start of our soft­ware, we patch the Microsoft Office execut­ables in memory. Rather than hard-cod­ing patch addresses, we search for small chunks of assembly code to be robust against minor changes in the execut­ables.

## How to apply

Please apply via this link: [Click here to apply](https://www.think-cell.com/career/jobs/development?ec=rC0IZep7h7pmLcx8ndavN6CYqAqwS1w6rWJ8C30Q0MQcBTL97qhrOxn34Sklr6jQNODy_edu-fd-bsHwp3MXAOL7NPAAMb1hHS7_WMeGegscny4d4DTH5RQfQqnnuy5IN6MX6_I)

After you apply through the link, our team will reach out to you through e-mail.
If you have any questions, please contact our team via e-mail: hr@think-cell.com

items.title: Working Group Two - infra/software engineer - Norway/Sweden/Germany/Remote
items.description:
**The team and your role**

We consist of driven engineers with a range of backgrounds – from global giants to small startups. Our team members work at all levels of our stack and have a large set of skills. Our company tech stack encompasses a wide variety of “cloud-native” tools and frameworks, including Kubernetes, Java, Kotlin, Go, Bazel, Envoy, AWS, and Terraform.  But if we can’t find the right tools, we build them!

The Infrastructure team at Working Group Two builds, maintains and runs the tools that support the engineering efforts of the rest of the company.  While many companies take infrastructure for granted, WG2 realizes that the infrastructure work we do is vital for our platform and is a big lever that enables security, speed, agility and scale in the other teams.  Because Working Group Two realizes that good infrastructure is a competitive advantage, we have the freedom to take a proactive approach and can plan longer-term and bigger tasks rather than only reacting to immediate needs.


We are now looking for an experienced infrastructure/software engineer with knowledge of cloud-based systems to join us and be part of our team building scalable infrastructure.  We believe you have experience with at least some of the following: 

- Writing distributed, well-designed services and you know what it means to ship high-quality code. 

- Building infrastructure to scale up and out. 

- Managing container-based workloads, using Kubernetes or other orchestration software. 

- AWS, or other cloud infrastructure providers. 

- Site-reliability engineering 


It is beneficial if you have experience managing, operating and upgrading multiple Kubernetes clusters in a safe and reliable manner.  Knowledge of networks in a Kubernetes environment is a plus.


**What will you do**

We work to make the business work; creating the frameworks, capabilities, and tools needed to welcome millions of users globally. The infrastructure team is responsible for the setup and management of our AWS accounts and Kubernetes clusters, running internal services, and building internal tools when we can't find something to meet our needs.

Our team may work on our Kubernetes clusters, CI/CD system, build dashboards and create alerts out of metrics, develop needed tools or help teams tune and secure their services.  We also help ensure services that need more esoteric protocols such as SCTP, RTP and DIAMETER function in a cloud environment.  WG2 follows the tenets of modern cloud infrastructure - we build and deploy our production systems in an immutable manner, from code. We believe in thorough metrics, monitoring, and alerting. We feel that our teams should own their services, but we in the infrastructure team should provide powerful tools to enable this.

This role is based in either of our locations in Stockholm, Oslo, Trondheim, remotely from Germany or other European countries.

**Our company culture**

If you join us, you’ll get the trust and freedom needed to make a big impact in the telco industry. Since we’re solving telco problems in a modern way, we recruit from various industries and have people that used to work at Telenor, Google, Microsoft, Spotify, AWS among others. We promise that you’ll be surrounded by friendly, motivated and highly skilled coworkers. We have a culture of transparency and openness, and oftentimes we call ourselves a “start-up for grown-ups”. We know where we’re going, and with a lot of trust in each other, we’ll pave the way there together.

We’re a distributed company with offices in Oslo, Trondheim and Stockholm, a hub in Germany, as well as some remote colleagues in other locations (a total of 8 countries). The Covid-19 pandemic forced a lot of companies to change how they think, but for us it confirmed that our remote-friendly and transparent approach goes a long way.

items.title: Spectrm - Senior Platform Engineer (DevOps & SRE) - Germany or remote (+/- 1 hr CET)
items.description:
Hey, it's my second job posting here :) Other than Backend Engineers, Spectrm is also looking for a Senior Platform Engineer to bring our conversational marketing platform to the next level. You may find out more here https://spectrm.io/careers/#job-1009639

• €75-100 k gross p.a.
• Full Time

## Location ##
• Germany or
• Remotely within +/- 1hr CEST

## What You'll Do ##

- Identify, document and execute initiatives to continuously improve Spectrm’s platform performance, scalability, reliability and maintainability.
- Develop tooling and practises to simplify operating the Spectrm’s platform.
- Improve the developers’ tooling and workflow, accelerating developers productivity and increasing their happiness, removing friction on their day-by-day tasks.
- Configure monitoring and alerting systems to proactively notify on symptoms and anomalies, preventing outages.
- Improve the deployment process to ensure resilience and reproducibility.
- Debug production issues across the entire stack, from infrastructure to end user experience, through all application layers.
- Document detailed post-mortem analysis of each and every incident, highlighting root cause and detailing the plan to implement preventive measures that will avoid the same incident in the future.
- Design, build and maintain the core infrastructure services that allow Spectrm to scale.
- Plan the growth of Spectrm’s infrastructure.
- Be on an on-call rotation to respond to Spectrm availability incidents, and provide support to engineers and to customer’s incidents. Use on-call shifts to proactively work on incident prevention.
- Lead by example, driving a culture of continuous improvement.

## What You Need to Be Successful ## 
Significant experience with Go and PHP

Must Have
• Go and PHP
• Previous experience as DevOps or SRE

Nice to Have
• Database or datawarehouse
• experience working in SaaS environment
• AWS
• Terraform
• Kubernetes
• Git
• CI/CD
• Observability

## About Our company ## 
Our mission is to empower creativity and elevate marketing performance for innovative brands. We believe the future of marketing is conversational and the opportunities are limitless. Help us build a world where marketing talks with people. Not at them. As the only global messaging partners of Meta and Google, we are looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to join our growing team.

## Perks ##
• Competitive salary and equity package
• Unlimited contracts
• Remote-first approach
• Quarterly onsite meetings full of fun and project planning activities
• A diverse multinational team
• 30 vacation days
• We work hard, but mental health and work-life balance is essential, so we work 4 days a week during summertime
• Opportunity to grow and learn with us
• Great office in one of the iconic Berlin buildings in the heart of Kreuzberg

## Please apply directly on https://spectrm.io/careers/#job-1051285, or email your CV to [jiun@spectrm.io]  ##
